campbell.title: Campbell City Council — April 21, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to discuss and vote on changes to how much local businesses pay for their annual licenses. Shannon Dragman from the finance department explained the proposed tax rate updates, and the council debated the changes in public before deciding on next steps.
keyTopics: Business license tax rates being increased or adjusted
Annual update to municipal code fees
Finance department recommendation on new rates
Public council discussion and voting on the changes

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 to get community input on a new rule or policy. After hearing from residents, the council moved forward by introducing an ordinance (a new city law) and skipped the usual first-reading step to speed up the process. The ordinance is scheduled to be officially adopted at the next council meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident feedback
New ordinance introduced and fast-tracked for adoption
First reading waived to accelerate the process
Final vote scheduled for next regular council meeting

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 21, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 21, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from their previous session and review the town's finances for February. The council authorized the Town Manager to sign several contracts for construction services and professional agreements to move forward with town improvement projects.
keyTopics: Approved previous meeting minutes from April 7
Reviewed February 2026 financial and investment report
Authorized construction contracts with QLM, Inc. and other service providers
Approved town improvement resolutions and planning

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 29, 2026
san-jose.summary: San José City Council held a meeting on April 29, 2026 to plan upcoming agendas for May. The council reviewed what items would be discussed at their May 5th and May 12th meetings, deciding which topics needed special time slots and checking whether any documents required special approval notices.
keyTopics: Finalized agenda items for May 5, 2026 meeting
Planned draft agenda for May 12, 2026 meeting
Public records review from mid-April
Time scheduling decisions for agenda items

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 30, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met to approve meeting minutes and hear directly from residents during public comment time. The main focus was deciding which neighborhood improvement projects should receive funding for the next fiscal year, with council members discussing their recommendations and attending status.
keyTopics: Neighborhood grant funding decisions for 2026-27 fiscal year
Public comment period for residents to speak directly to council
Council attendance and participation in neighborhood meetings
Review and approval of previous meeting minutes

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 28, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special study session on April 28, 2026 to interview candidates for various city boards and commissions. Residents were able to participate both in person and online to observe the interview process and provide public comment.
keyTopics: Board and commission candidate interviews
Public comment from residents (in-person and online)
Study session format for vetting board applicants

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 30, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met on April 30, 2026 to approve meeting notes from the Economic Development Committee's January and March sessions. The council also opened the floor for residents to speak about any topics under the committee's authority for up to three minutes each.
keyTopics: Approval of January 29, 2026 Economic Development Committee minutes
Approval of March 27, 2026 Economic Development Committee minutes
Public comment period on economic development matters

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 21, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council met to discuss allowing large promotional signs to be temporarily installed on buildings near Levi's Stadium during the 2026 FIFA World Cup. The council also approved a new three-year contract with the firefighters union covering 2026-2029, though one agenda item was withdrawn before the meeting.
keyTopics: Temporary supergraphic signs approved near Levi's Stadium for World Cup 2026
New firefighter union contract approved for 2026-2029
One agenda item withdrawn by city staff before discussion

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 21, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held a meeting on April 21, 2026 to address several important financial and community issues. They discussed approving a tax responsibility act, setting fees and charges for the next fiscal year, and received an update on efforts to address homelessness in Santa Clara County.
keyTopics: Tax Equity and Fiscal Responsibility Act reissuance
FY 2026-27 budget and development fees
User fees and service charges updates
Santa Clara County homelessness prevention program
Approval of previous meeting minutes and upcoming calendar

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 29, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to consider a proposal to divide a vacant 1-acre lot on Old Trace Road into nine separate residential lots that would allow construction of nine new single-family homes plus seven smaller accessory dwelling units. The council also handled routine business including electing leadership positions and reviewing the director's report and upcoming meeting schedule.
keyTopics: Subdivision plan for 4103 Old Trace Road creating 9 single-family homes + 7 accessory units
Project is exempt from lengthy environmental review under state law
Addition of new private street to access the nine new residential properties
Council leadership elections (Chair and Vice Chair positions)
